matches.x: HEN there was a famine: in: the days of David three years, year after year; and David inquired. of the Lord. And the Lord answered, It is.for Saul, and for his: bloody. house, because he slew the Gibeonites.

R. Chana b. Adda said: "The decree concerning the Gibeonites was issued by David, as it is said (II Sam. 21, 2) And the king called for the Gibeonites and said unto them: now the Gibeonites are not of the children of Israel." Why did David make such a decree against them? Because, as it is written And then was a famine in the days of David three years, year after year. During the first year of…

3. When David was gone a little farther, there met him Ziba, the servant
of Mephibosheth, [whom he had sent to take care of the possessions which
had been given him, as the son of Jonathan, the son of Saul,] with a
couple of asses, loaden with provisions, and desired him to take as much
of them as he and his followers stood in need of. And when the king
asked him where he had left Mephibosheth,…

The name Michal must be a mistake for Merab, for she was the daughter of Saul who had been given to Adriel (2 Sam. 21:8; 1 Sam. 18:19). If they were indeed sons of Michal, or even if they were sons of Merab whom Michal had "brought up" for Adriel, that was one more sad episode in the relationship of David and Michal. (Ellis T. Rasmussen, A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old Testament, p.271)

Rasmussen, A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old Testament, p.271)
